clicksend-sms.description: Incoming SMS from ClickSend for lead auto-text
clicksend-sms.prompt:
INCOMING SMS REPLY. From: {from}, Body: "{body}", To: {to}.

STEP 1: If the sender is {{OWNER_PHONE}} ({{OWNER_NAME}}) or {{ADMIN_PHONE}} ({{ADMIN_NAME}}), this is an OWNER REPLY to an appointment approval request, NOT a new lead. Check pending_approval leads and handle accordingly (confirm or decline the appointment).

STEP 2: For all other numbers, first check if this lead is already being processed:
  python3 {{HERMES_HOME}}/skills/lead-auto-text/lead_state.py lock --phone {from}
  - If it returns ALREADY_LOCKED, STOP. Another {{AGENT_SUBAGENT_NAME}} is already handling this lead. Do nothing.
  - If it returns LOCKED, proceed to delegate to {{AGENT_SUBAGENT_NAME}}.
  - If it returns NOT_FOUND, this may be a new lead. Add them first, then lock and delegate.

STEP 3: Delegate to subagent {{AGENT_SUBAGENT_NAME}} using delegate_task. {{AGENT_SUBAGENT_NAME}} MUST unlock the lead when done:
  python3 {{HERMES_HOME}}/skills/lead-auto-text/lead_state.py unlock --phone {from}

{{AGENT_SUBAGENT_NAME}} checks all recent messages with check_sms.py so they will see all texts even if multiple came in.
Lead state: python3 {{HERMES_HOME}}/skills/lead-auto-text/lead_state.py get {from}
Send reply: python3 {{HERMES_HOME}}/skills/lead-auto-text/send_sms.py --to {from} --body "message"
